title: Class action lawsuit against Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron for RAM price fixing causes historic price surge
summary: Chip manufacturers are accused of collusion to artificially increase DRAM memory prices, raising the costs of kits like EXPO ULL by up to 80%.

title: ZLUDA allows running CUDA and PhysX applications on AMD GPUs with up to 300% performance
summary: The open source project ZLUDA achieves compatibility with CUDA and PhysX physics acceleration on Radeon, although it faces funding issues.

title: Nintendo Switch 2 could have a new Sharp LCD panel in some revisions
summary: Leaks indicate that the console will feature a better quality LCD screen in certain batches, generating uncertainty among buyers.

title: AMD confirms low-power cores in future Zen 6 CPUs similar to Intel's LPE
summary: Linux patches reveal that Zen 6 processors will include a new type of efficient core for background tasks.

title: Sony advances plans for a more powerful PS6, a possible portable console and a high price
summary: The company acknowledges that memory cost is a problem and suggests that PlayStation hardware will not be sold at a loss, fueling rumors of a 1000 euro PS6 and a handheld.

title: Cursor launches beta version for iOS with AI agents and repository features
summary: The AI code editing platform lands on mobile, although it generates controversy over privacy changes when installed.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5, a faster and cheaper model that brings performance closer to Opus
summary: The new version of the AI assistant offers competitive capabilities at a lower cost, facilitating its use in autonomous agents.

title: Anthropic presents Claude Science, an AI workspace for scientists and researchers
summary: The tool focuses on research workflows and is now available on Microsoft Foundry with Azure integration.

title: Google introduces Nano Banana 2 Lite, a faster and more economical image generator
summary: The new AI image generation model is launched alongside Gemini Omni Flash for video, available via API.

title: Meta conducted secret tests with competitor chatbots using profiles of minors
summary: The company used contractors to pose as teenagers and verify the responses of ChatGPT, Gemini and Character.AI in mental health crises.

title: Taiwan raids offices of Supermicro and partners for alleged smuggling of Nvidia chips to China
summary: Authorities searched nine locations and interrogated six people in an investigation into illegal semiconductor exports.

title: Cargo thieves steal data center supplies worth $1.3 million
summary: Police recovered near Chicago copper cable and stolen equipment, evidencing the growing black market for AI infrastructure materials.

title: NVIDIA rumored to have cancelled or indefinitely delayed the 9 GB RTX 5050
summary: Graphics card manufacturers have no new information about the model, increasing uncertainty about its launch.

title: SteamOS 3.8.20 beta improves VRAM management and updates the Mesa driver
summary: The new version of Valve's operating system optimizes performance on consoles and gaming laptops, including improved Ray Tracing support.

title: Creator converts an e-ink development board into a functional 60 Hz Game Boy
summary: Using an ESP32-S3 microcontroller and a 960x540 display, it manages to emulate retro games without needing expensive components.

title: AMD warns its partners of a price increase on Radeon GPUs and memory packages in July
summary: The increase would be around 10% due to rising component costs and high AI demand.

title: Thousands of Xbox employees unionize as historic mass layoffs are feared
summary: More than 3500 workers join the CWA union to protect their jobs, while experts warn of possible massive cuts in Microsoft's gaming division.

title: A county with 37 data centers asks schools to save electricity due to rising costs
summary: Henrico County, Virginia, anticipates a 25% increase in electricity rates and recommends measures such as turning off computers and lowering blinds.

title: Meituan presents LongCat-2.0, a massive AI model that shows China can train without Nvidia
summary: The 1.6 trillion parameter MoE model competes with the best US systems, bypassing chip export restrictions.

title: Tesla begins testing its Cybercab without steering wheel or pedals in Austin
summary: The company's autonomous vehicle begins testing on public roads in Texas, marking a step towards driverless transportation.
